items.body:
## Summary

A broken `pnpm-lock.yaml` introduced by the automatic pnpm-update commit ([a6df5fe](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/a6df5fe2c55c89a99c83575b9e9ebd004fc0c4e0) on Apr 13 2026) is causing every CI pipeline on `main` to fail. The lockfile importer section for `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth` references `vitest@4.1.2` and `@vitest/coverage-istanbul@4.1.2`, but the snapshots section only contains entries for `4.1.4`. All 421 other packages were correctly updated to `4.1.4`. Since every CI pipeline runs `pnpm install` against the shared lockfile, every pipeline fails at the install step.

Inspected CI runs on commit `b3fde7a3dc6d25c904b980378044a28b4f88c114` (Apr 14 2026 — the most recent commit with completed CI). **186 CI pipeline jobs** (Build UnitTest + Build Analyze) are failing across 20+ service directories including `notificationhubs`, `search`, `iot`, `template`, `fluidrelay-mgmt`, `applicationinsights-mgmt`, `eventhub-mgmt`, `datafactory-mgmt`, and many more.

## Failures

### All packages (systemic — `pnpm install` fails for the whole workspace)

**Failing tests:** N/A — CI pipelines fail before any tests run.

**Error (from CI annotations):**
```
ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_MISSING_DEPENDENCY  Broken lockfile: no entry for
'`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`@4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)' in pnpm-lock.yaml

This issue is probably caused by a badly resolved merge conflict.
To fix the lockfile, run 'pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile'.
```

Confirmed locally — running `pnpm install` at the repo root fails immediately with this error.

**Root cause:**

The `pnpm update` PR ([a6df5fe](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/a6df5fe2c55c89a99c83575b9e9ebd004fc0c4e0)) bumped `vitest`/`@vitest/coverage-istanbul` from `4.1.2` → `4.1.4` for all 421 workspace packages and updated the snapshots section accordingly (removing `4.1.2` snapshot entries and adding `4.1.4`). However, it failed to update the importer section for `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth`, which still references:

- `@vitest/coverage-istanbul`: `version: 4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)` (no such snapshot exists)
- `vitest`: `version: 4.1.2(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.2)(vite@7.3.1(...))` (no such snapshot exists)

Because `pnpm install` runs for the entire workspace in every CI pipeline (no per-package filter), this single stale lockfile entry breaks every pipeline.

**Suggested fix:**

```diff
--- a/pnpm-lock.yaml
+++ b/pnpm-lock.yaml
@@ sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth devDependencies @@
       '`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`':
         specifier: catalog:testing
-        version: 4.1.2(vitest@4.1.2)
+        version: 4.1.4(vitest@4.1.4)
 ...
       vitest:
         specifier: catalog:testing
-        version: 4.1.2(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.2)(vite@7.3.1(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(terser@5.46.1)(tsx@4.21.0)(yaml@2.8.3))
+        version: 4.1.4(`@opentelemetry/api`@1.9.1)(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(`@vitest/browser-playwright`@4.1.4)(`@vitest/coverage-istanbul`@4.1.4)(vite@7.3.1(`@types/node`@20.19.39)(terser@5.46.1)(tsx@4.21.0)(yaml@2.8.3))
```

The `4.1.4` snapshot entries already exist at lines 41609 and 45097 of the current lockfile, so no new snapshots need to be added.

Alternatively, regenerate the lockfile by running `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile` at the repo root (which requires the private Azure DevOps registry to be accessible).

**Reproduced locally:** Yes — `pnpm install` fails with `ERR_PNPM_LOCKFILE_MISSING_DEPENDENCY` on the current `main`.

---

## Additional Notes

- The "After APIView" check runs that appear as failures on the `b9e6755` commit are unrelated — those are GitHub Actions failures for API review on PRs from forked repos and do not represent test failures.
- The CI test-proxy log errors (`Path does not exist: .../test-proxy.log`) are a downstream symptom of the `pnpm install` failure — pipelines fail before the test-proxy is started.
- The broken entry is in the `sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth` importer section at lines ~23503–23526 of `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
items.title: CI failures on main: broken pnpm-lock.yaml causes widespread pnpm install failures
